texto: Marriage and family life give us constant opportunities to deny ourselves for the sake of others. And yet self-denial is not a mask for self-contempt, but the necessary means for achieving self-mastery; for self-mastery makes possible our self-giving and self-fulfillment. Sin is not wanting too much, but settling for too little. It's settling for self-gratification rather than self-fulfillment.
explicacao: Citação de Scott Hahn sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Marriage and family life give us constant opportunities to deny ourselves for the sake of others.…” articula familia, family, marriage em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como casamento e marriage.

Sin is not wanting too much, but settling for too little. It's settling for self-gratification rather than self-fulfillment.

texto: Judgment, then, is not an impersonal, legalistic process. It is a matter of love, and it is something we choose for ourselves. Nor is punishment a vindictive act. God's "curses" are not expressions of hatred, but of fatherly love and discipline. Like medicinal ointment, they hurt in order to heal. They impose suffering that is remedial, restorative, and redemptive. God's wrath is an expression of His love for His wayward children.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Scott Hahn: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Judgment, then, is not an impersonal, legalistic process. It is a matter of love, and it is somet…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, father, god pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de god e judgment.

texto: What's wrong with the world?It's easy to probe the ills of the nation, the Church, and the planet and come up with a grave diagnosis... But it takes all the strength we can muster to stand at Mass and honestly say, 'I have greatly sinned, in my thoughts and in my words, in what I have done and what I have failed to do..."Sin is not out there; it's deep inside you and me... What's wrong with the world? I am, because I sin, and my sins well up from the darkness in my own heart.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Scott Hahn que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “What's wrong with the world?It's easy to probe the ills of the nation, the Church, and the planet…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, confession, sin, com foco em reflexao-noturna e sin.

texto: Faith and reason are indeed complementary faculties that we use to think about the truth. When any winged creature (or mechanism) tries to fly on just one wing, it falls to the ground. In a similar way, when we human beings try to wing it with just one faculty, we crash.
